result.markdown:
# Change in Mean (Example 1)



We first simulate a white-noise time series of length 20,000 with 20 change
points and 21 piecewise-constant mean regimes. The noise variance is fixed, so
this example focuses on changes in the mean.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
set.seed(1234)

n <- 20000

change_points <- c(
  952, 1905, 2858, 3810, 4763, 5715, 6668, 7620, 8573, 9525,
  10478, 11430, 12383, 13335, 14288, 15240, 16193, 17145, 18098,
  19050
)

means <- c(
  0, 2, -1, 3, 0.5, -2, 2, 5, -0.5, 2.5, 0, -2.5, -1.5, 1.5,
  3, 1, 0, 1.25, -2, 3.5, -1.5
)

segment_starts <- c(1L, change_points + 1L)
segment_ends <- c(change_points, n)
x_mean <- numeric(n)

for (j in seq_along(means)) {
  segment_index <- segment_starts[j]:segment_ends[j]
  x_mean[segment_index] <- rnorm(length(segment_index), mean = means[j], sd = 1)
}

change_points
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
 [1]   952  1905  2858  3810  4763  5715  6668  7620  8573  9525 10478 11430
[13] 12383 13335 14288 15240 16193 17145 18098 19050
```


:::
:::


The plot below shows the simulated series.


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](change-in-mean_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-2-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Run SCAN Change-Point Detection

The `window_sizes` argument controls the local scales used by the scan. The
window sizes should be smaller than the spacing between nearby change points,
while still being large enough to estimate the local distributions on each side
of a candidate split.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fit_mean <- scan_cpd(
  x_mean,
  window_sizes = c(100, 164, 227, 291, 355, 418, 482, 546, 609, 673, 737),
  n_boot = 400,
  random_state = 1234,
  change_type = "mean",
  n_jobs = 1
)

fit_mean
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
scanr change-point result
observations: 20000 
change points: 952, 1905, 2858, 3809, 4760, 5715, 6668, 7620, 8573, 9525, 10478, 11410, 12380, 13338, 14288, 15230, 16177, 17145, 18098, 19050 
```


:::
:::


::: {.callout-note}
## Remark on Bootstrap Calibration

The adaptive threshold for declaring a change point is derived through a
**tapered block bootstrap** across the combined windows. Increasing the number
of bootstrap samples (`n_boot`) produces a more accurate null distribution at
the cost of longer computation time; a value of 400-1,000 is typically
sufficient. The method supports parallel processing via
[Rust's](https://www.rust-lang.org/) **Rayon** library for computational
efficiency.
:::

The estimated change points can be extracted directly from the fitted object.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
fit_mean$change_points
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
 [1]   952  1905  2858  3809  4760  5715  6668  7620  8573  9525 10478 11410
[13] 12380 13338 14288 15230 16177 17145 18098 19050
```


:::
:::


## Evaluate Detection Accuracy

For simulated data, estimated change points can be compared with the known
truth. The `tolerance` argument controls how close an estimated change point
must be to a true change point to count as a match.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
cpd_metrics(
  true_cps = change_points,
  estimated_cps = fit_mean$change_points,
  n = length(x_mean),
  tolerance = 20
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
$matches
    true estimated distance
1    952       952        0
2   1905      1905        0
3   2858      2858        0
4   5715      5715        0

10 14288     14288        0
11 17145     17145        0
12 18098     18098        0
13 19050     19050        0
14  3810      3809        1
15  4763      4760        3
16 12383     12380        3
17 13335     13338        3
18 15240     15230       10
19 16193     16177       16
20 11430     11410       20

$precision
[1] 1

$recall
[1] 1

$f1
[1] 1

$covering
[1] 0.9944529
```


:::
:::


## Visualize the Result

The package includes visual helpers for inspecting detected change points,
window-level votes, and the overall vote scree.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vis_change_points(
  x_mean,
  fit_mean,
  true_change_points = change_points,
  x_label = "Time",
  y_label = "Value"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](change-in-mean_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Tune the Voting Threshold

The ensemble vote threshold, `vote_threshold`, controls how much agreement is
required across window sizes before a candidate is retained. The default value
is 0.5, meaning that a candidate must be supported by at least half of the
available window-level votes.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vis_vote_scree(fit_mean)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](change-in-mean_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
vis_window_votes(fit_mean)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](change-in-mean_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::
